published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2018OptimalInference gives fixed linear-functional inference over convex smoothness classes and KolesarRothe2018DiscreteRD gives fixed-cutoff discrete-running-variable honest inference; neither subsumes a cost-quality selected cascade cutoff, but the interval equality is close to their fixed-target endpoint machinery.

Kotte2026UCCI selects an isotonic calibrated routing threshold by constrained cost minimization and Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt characterizes deterministic cascade frontiers; neither identifies or bounds the selected local boundary value V_*.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1 / sharp RD manipulation bounds from a McCrary envelope and covariate-share caps has adjacent endpoint-kink and binding-constraint geometry, but not selected cascade cutoffs.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2020SimpleHonest indexes honest intervals by smoothness budgets but does not give finite-atom active-set geometry for a selected cutoff endpoint.

ArmstrongKolesar2018BandwidthSnooping, BachocPreinerstorferSteinberger2016PostSelection, CalonicoCattaneoFarrell2018OptimalBandwidthRBC, DongKolesar2021RunningVariableMeasurementError, and Pernot2023IsotonicStratification are adjacent selection, RD, generated-running-variable, or isotonic-score precedents, not a generated-score selected-threshold CI for this cascade target.

soundness_flags.label: target_estimand / V_*(P)
soundness_flags.one_line: V_*(P)=mu_P(tau_*(P)) is not a well-defined functional of the observed-data law in the atomic regime because mu_P is a nonunique smooth representative; make the primitive target set-valued [L_*,U_*] or explicitly enlarge the latent model to include mu.

soundness_flags.label: ass:population-feasible-threshold
soundness_flags.one_line: Kotte2026UCCI supports constrained threshold selection, but feasibility of this exact population quality-constrained threshold set is a problem-specific regularity condition rather than a standard assumption under that cite.

soundness_flags.label: ass:local-two-sided-atom-support
soundness_flags.one_line: KolesarRothe2018DiscreteRD supports discrete running-variable inference, but bracketing positive-mass atoms around an endogenously selected cascade cutoff is not a standard condition stated by that paper.

soundness_flags.label: ass:score-cef-extension
soundness_flags.one_line: ArmstrongKolesar2018OptimalInference assumes regression functions lie in convex smoothness classes, not a standard existential C^2 interpolation representative through atomic score regressions at a selected cutoff.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:selected-sharp-set / main identification kernel
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The sharp interval is conditional on imposing a range-respecting C^2 curvature-budget extension class.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The selected endpoint interval is a field-tier sharp bound under the stated smooth-extension model, not an unrestricted observed-law identification result.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:quadratic-selection-growth / dense selected threshold
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Quadratic feasible-argmin growth is accepted as a conditional local regularity premise for the dense inference handle.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The post-selection CI conjecture is bound to laws with isolated selected-cutoff regularity.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:postselect-honest-ci / generated-score drift
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The inference rung is conditional on constructing the data-only hat G_n drift envelope and selected-threshold critical value.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Coverage is an open dense-regime handle, not a settled theorem for atomic isotonic-score supports.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Rewrite the primitive target as the set-valued functional [L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M)] or explicitly define the full latent object as (P, mu), so V_* is no longer presented as a unique observed-law functional.
Retag selected-threshold feasibility, bracketing-atom support, and C^2 interpolation as proposal-specific regularity conditions, with direct plausibility arguments for calibrated cascade scores.
Add a small finite-atom worked example computing L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M), and M_*(P) from primitive score atoms to make the critical-curvature object more auditable.

Sharpen Proposition 1 by isolating the non-representability claim as a named corollary against Kotte2026UCCI and Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt, with the same-law ambiguity stated only for non-singleton endpoint sets.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle; the kernel is plausibly field-new, but the target functional and assumption tags need semantic repair before Stage 0.
